electroacoustic.frequency_range_hz: 32 Hz to 60/100 Hz (LF limit -10 dB at 32 Hz; upper limit set by preset)
electroacoustic.directivity: Omnidirectional in standard configuration; rear-rejection (cardioid) available via dedicated presets and a reversed element
electroacoustic.components: 1 x 18 inch neodymium direct-radiation cone driver in a dual bass-reflex enclosure, one vent using a progressive-profile L-Vent for laminar low-turbulence airflow

connection.input_connector: 2x 4-point speakON (NL4): one IN and one LINK; LINK carries the same signal in parallel for daisy-chaining a second cabinet (parallel linking only supported on LA8/LA12X). Internal pinout uses points 1+/1- for the LF driver; points 2+/2- unused
connection.breakouts: DOSUB-LA8 (8-point PA-COM to 4x 2-point speakON) splits one LA8 PA-COM output to four cabinets; SP-Y1 (4-point speakON to 2x 2-point speakON, supplied with CC4FP adapter) splits a paired output to two cabinets

physical.cabinet: Premium-grade Baltic beech and birch plywood; coated steel front grille with Airnet acoustically neutral 3D fabric; dark grey-brown Pantone 426 C finish
physical.handling: 4 handles, 8 protective corner stops, 35 mm pole-mount socket

modes: Ground-stacked (floor-stacked logo-down, or platform-stacked on M-BUMP with M-JACK feet)
Flown (vertical SB18 array, or mixed vertical SB18/Kara array, via M-BUMP or KARA-MINIBU)
Pole-mounted top enclosure (XT/X or Kiva via the 35 mm socket)
deployment.cardioid_array: Cardioid configuration uses a block of 4 subwoofers with 1 reversed element (reversed cabinet to OUT 1)
deployment.standard_spacing: Place enclosures side by side; if separated, max distance between adjacent acoustic centers is 1.7 m (SB18_100 presets) or 2.8 m (SB18_60 presets)
rigging_accessories: M-BUMP (flying/stacking frame for vertical SB18 and/or Kara arrays)
M-JACK (set of 4 platform feet for M-BUMP)
KARA-MINIBU (compact flying/stacking frame for small SB18/Kara arrays)
deployment.rigging_note: Maximum flown/stacked array size is not fixed by a single number; all configurations must be validated in SOUNDVISION with no stress or stability warning (compliant with BGV-C1 2012 and EN ISO 12100-1)

usage: Reference 18-inch subwoofer for extending L-Acoustics WST line sources (Kara, Kudo) and coaxial enclosures (X/XT, Kiva) down to a 32 Hz low-frequency limit.
Needs one amplifier channel per cabinet at 8 ohms; an LA4X drives up to 4, an LA12X up to 12, an LA7.16 up to 6, and an LA2Xi up to 4 (single-ended) or 2 (bridged).
Pick the upper crossover by preset: SB18_100 (100 Hz, also the spec/Max-SPL reference) for tighter sub/main alignment, or SB18_60 (60 Hz) when handing off lower to the mains.
Deploy four cabinets with one reversed and a cardioid preset (_C / _Cx) to achieve rear SPL rejection and keep low end off the stage.
Mount flown or ground-stacked using M-BUMP or KARA-MINIBU hardware, standalone or interleaved with a Kara line array; the pole socket lets it carry a top XT/X or Kiva box.

Daisy-chain a second cabinet from the LINK speakON only when driven by an LA8 or LA12X; keep side-by-side spacing within 1.7 m (100 Hz) or 2.8 m (60 Hz) acoustic-center limits when boxes cannot touch.
source_manual: SB18 / SB18i / SB18m Subwoofer User Manual (EN), v6.0; with SB18 Rigging Manual (EN) v2.0 and SB18 Product Specification Sheet (EN) 7.0
